reasoning: The resolution depends on whether Jorden Van Foreest's Elo rating on April 5, 2026 (the official April rating list from FIDE) is at least 1% higher than his rating on March 29, 2026. The baseline rating was 2729, which means a 1% increase requires him to gain ~27.29 points within the month to securely reach a rating of ~2756.3. Information from the Prague Masters 2026 tournament (Feb 24 - Mar 6) shows that while Van Foreest started out exceptionally strong, accumulating 5/7 in the first 7 rounds and peaking at 11th in the live ratings (around ~2748-2750), he lost both of his final two games (Rounds 8 and 9) against Aravindh Chithambaram and Parham Maghsoodloo. Consequently, his final score was +1 (5/9), which is expected to yield only a very small gain of a few points for a 2729-rated player. Even factoring in a smaller earlier match against Ediz Gurel (+~6 points), his total live rating for the upcoming April list would safely settle around 2735-2740, significantly short of the 2756.3 threshold. Gaining another ~18 points later in March is implausible. Therefore, the probability of meeting the 1% increase benchmark is extremely low.
